name: PayFin — Payout Engine
summary: Multi-bank orchestration engine with smart routing, failover, IFT cost optimization, and prefunding distribution.
description: A horizontally-scaled payout platform that abstracts partner-specific quirks behind a unified contract. Smart router picks the cheapest healthy rail per transaction; each bank has its own sliding-window throttle and TPS contract.
features: Multi-bank smart routing with health checks
Queue-based status polling with exponential backoff

name: UPI Pay-in / Payout APIs
summary: Production UPI APIs powering merchant transaction flows with idempotency, callbacks, and reconciliation.
description: Core UPI rails for merchant pay-in and payout. Idempotency keys prevent duplicates; callbacks and reconciliation hooks ensure consistent ledger state across systems.
architecture: Go / Java APIs · PostgreSQL · Redis cache · webhook queues

name: PayFin — Pay-in Checkout
summary: End-to-end checkout supporting UPI, Cards, Netbanking, Wallets with success-probability ranking.
description: Merchant-facing checkout that parallelizes bank calls and precomputes payment preferences. Multi-acquirer abstraction enables rapid onboarding of new bank partners.
architecture: Go backend · Next.js / React frontend · PostgreSQL · Redis cache

name: Wallet & Liquidity Engine
summary: Centralized wallet with Redis distributed locking and continuous liquidity rebalancing across partner banks.
description: Strict-consistency wallet under high concurrency. Continuously rebalances prefunded capital across partners based on real-time utilization to maximize throughput availability.
features: Fencing tokens to prevent stale writes

name: ChitChaat — CPaaS Backend
summary: High-throughput messaging across SMS, WhatsApp, RCS using gRPC and Kafka with automated provider failover.
description: Multi-channel messaging backend with provider abstraction, automated failover, and a top-up/credit system for managing customer messaging balances.
architecture: Go services · gRPC · Kafka · Redis state · PostgreSQL

name: Transactional Messaging Backend
summary: SMS notification backend tied to payment events with provider failover and delivery tracking.
description: Lightweight, event-driven SMS dispatcher that subscribes to payment events and guarantees customer communication even during provider outages.
architecture: Event consumer · provider abstraction · DLQ for failed sends
